message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested a comprehensive exploration and analysis of a Django REST Framework + React codebase called "Duality Pole Studio" to understand its structure. The user explicitly asked for:
   - Analysis of the payments app models, views, and serializers to identify supported payment methods and the API endpoint structure for creating payments
   - Examination of specific lines in the enrolments views (600-700 and 960-1000) to understand the enrolment list view and how cancellation works
   - Location and full content of `SeasonEnrolmentsPage.jsx`
   - Location and content of any `SeasonDetailPage.jsx` variant
   - Search for "view_roster\|View Roster\|viewRoster" usage in the frontend
   - Listing of API files and enumeration of payment/enrolment API calls
   - Final deliverable: file paths, key model fields, API endpoint URLs, and full content of SeasonEnrolmentsPage.jsx

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Django REST Framework with ListCreateAPIView, RetrieveAPIView, RetrieveUpdateAPIView patterns
   - React component-based architecture with API client calls
   - Payment processing with Stripe integration
   - Enrolment management with multiple enrolment types (trial, casual, course, catchup)
   - Season-based class structure with booking management
   - Model relationships: Payment → User, Enrolment → ClassSession → Season
   - API patterns: REST endpoints with list/create/update/delete operations
   - Cancellation offers and refund handling mechanisms
   - Payment methods: Credit/Debit Card (Stripe), Cash, Bank Transfer, Account Credit, Other

3. Files and Code Sections:
   
   `/home/user/test/backend/apps/payments/models.py`:
   - Contains Payment model with PaymentMethod choices: CARD, CASH, BANK (bank_transfer), ACCOUNT (account_credit), OTHER
   - PaymentType choices: PAYMENT, CHARGE, REFUND, CREDIT, NO_SHOW_FEE
   - Key fields: student (FK to User), payment_type, amount, description, reference, payment_method, stripe_payment_intent_id, created_by, created_at, admin_notes
   - Cash promise tracking fields: cash_promised_date, cash_received, cash_reminder_sent_at, cash_auto_charge_at
   - Related models: PaymentPlan, PaymentPlanInstalment, Package, StudentPackage, MembershipType, StudentMembership, GiftCard, PromoCode, CancellationOffer, PaymentChase
   
   `/home/user/test/backend/apps/payments/views.py`:
   - PaymentListView (line 20): ListCreateAPIView at `/api/payments/` allowing GET/POST for authenticated users
   - perform_create method checks user role and creates Payment with created_by=request.user
   - PaymentDetailView: RetrieveAPIView for retrieving individual payments
   - Stripe integration views: StripePaymentIntentView, StripeSetupIntentView, StripeChargeSavedCardView
   - CancellationOfferListView, MyCancellationOffersView, resolve_cancellation_offer endpoint
   - PaymentChaseListCreateView (line 713): handles payment chase notifications with steps (FRIENDLY, FIRM, FINAL)
   - CashPromiseView (line 792): admin endpoint to mark cash received or send reminders with 24h auto-charge timer
   
   `/home/user/test/backend/apps/payments/serializers.py`:
   - PaymentSerializer exposes fields: id, student, student_name, payment_type, amount, description, reference, payment_method, stripe_payment_intent_id, created_by, created_by_name, created_at, admin_notes, cash_promised_date, cash_received, cash_reminder_sent_at, cash_auto_charge_at
   - PaymentPlanSerializer with nested instalments
   - CancellationOfferSerializer with student_name, occurrence_label, session_name, occurrence_date
   
   `/home/user/test/backend/apps/payments/urls.py`:
   - POST/GET `/api/payments/` → PaymentListView
   - GET `/api/payments/<id>/` → PaymentDetailView
   - POST/GET `/api/payments/plans/` → PaymentPlanListView
   - POST `/api/payments/stripe/payment-intent/` → StripePaymentIntentView
   - POST/GET `/api/payments/cancellation-offers/` → CancellationOfferListView
   - POST `/api/payments/occurrences/<occurrence_pk>/send-cancellation-offers/` → send_cancellation_offers
   - POST `/api/payments/cash-promises/<pk>/action/` → CashPromiseView
   
   `/home/user/test/backend/apps/enrolments/views.py`:
   - EnrolmentListView (line 176): ListCreateAPIView for GET/POST to `/api/enrolments/`
   - get_queryset filters by student role or allows admin/instructor to filter by student_id, session_id, status
   - perform_create validates one trial per lifetime, checks season booking gates, catch-up cutoff weeks, required forms, booking blocks
   - Lines 600-700 contain FlaggedEnrolmentsView, PendingTrialFeedbackView, SubmitTrialFeedbackView
   - Lines 960-1000 show class change request handling: request_type == CANCEL branch cancels enrolment with refund_action options ('credit' or 'stripe')
   - Cancellation creates Payment record with PaymentType.CREDIT or initiates Stripe refund
   
   `/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/SeasonEnrolmentsPage.jsx`:
   - FILE DOES NOT EXIST - search returned no results
   
   `/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminSeasonDetail.jsx`:
   - Form for creating/editing seasons with fields: name, start_date, weeks (duration), status (upcoming/active/completed), go_live_at (datetime), notes
   - Discount tiers UI: array of {pos, amt} for incremental pricing tiers (2nd class, 3rd class, etc.)
   - Dropdown for position (ordinal: 2nd through 10th class) and input for dollar discount amount
   - POST to `seasons.create()` or PATCH to `seasons.update(id)`
   - Archive and Delete operations in danger zone
   - Key functions: calcEndDate() calculates Monday-to-Sunday week range, isoToLocalInput/localInputToISO for datetime conversion
   
   `/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminSeasonOverview.jsx`:
   - Component displays season details with KPIs: classes count, total enrolled/capacity, waitlisted count, weeks left
   - ClassRow component (line 72) shows individual session cards with enrolment progress bar
   - "View Roster" button (line 121) links to `/admin/classes/${session.id}/attendance`
   - Booking controls: toggle bookings open/closed, toggle enrolments enabled/disabled, close season
   - Classes sorted by day_of_week and start_time
   
   `/home/user/test/frontend/src/api/index.js`:
   - Enrolment API calls:
     - `enrolments.list(params)` → GET `/api/enrolments/`
     - `enrolments.create(data)` → POST `/api/enrolments/`
     - `enrolments.update(id, data)` → PATCH `/api/enrolments/${id}/`
     - `enrolments.delete(id)` → DELETE `/api/enrolments/${id}/`
     - `enrolments.changeRequests.approve(id, data)` → POST `/api/enrolments/change-requests/${id}/approve/`
   - Payment API calls:
     - `payments.list(params)` → GET `/api/payments/`
     - `payments.create(data)` → POST `/api/payments/`
     - `payments.stripe.createPaymentIntent(data)` → POST `/api/payments/stripe/payment-intent/`
     - `payments.plans.create(data)` → POST `/api/payments/plans/`
     - `payments.cancellationOffers.resolve(id, choice)` → POST `/api/payments/cancellation-offers/${id}/resolve/`
   - Season API calls:
     - `seasons.list()` → GET `/api/classes/seasons/`
     - `seasons.get(id)` → GET `/api/classes/seasons/${id}/`
     - `seasons.create(data)` → POST `/api/classes/seasons/`
     - `seasons.update(id, data)` → PATCH `/api/classes/seasons/${id}/`
   - Class API calls:
     - `classes.roster(sessionId)` → GET `/api/classes/sessions/${sessionId}/roster/`
     - `classes.seasonEnrolments(sessionId)` → GET `/api/classes/sessions/${sessionId}/season-enrolments/`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - No errors were encountered. The exploration was completed successfully without requiring troubleshooting or corrections.

5. Problem Solving:
   - No problems encountered. The codebase was successfully explored and examined as requested.

6. All user messages:
   - Initial request: "I need to understand the codebase structure for a Django REST Framework + React app called Duality Pole Studio. Please look at these specific things and report back concisely: 1. `/home/user/test/backend/apps/payments/` - what models/views/serializers exist? Specifically what payment methods are supported (Stripe, cash, bank transfer, Square?) and what does the API endpoint look like for creating a payment? 2. `/home/user/test/backend/apps/enrolments/views.py` lines 600-700 and around line 960-1000 - what does the enrolment list view look like, and how does cancellation work (what fields does it accept)? 3. `/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/SeasonEnrolmentsPage.jsx` - what's currently in this file (full content)? 4. `/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/admin/SeasonDetailPage.jsx` OR `/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/SeasonDetailPage.jsx` - does this exist? If so, what does it look like? 5. Grep for "view_roster\|View Roster\|viewRoster" in the frontend to find where it's used. 6. `/home/user/test/frontend/src/api/` - list the files and check what payment/enrolment API calls exist. Report: file paths, key model fields, API endpoint URLs, and the full content of SeasonEnrolmentsPage.jsx."
   - Final instruction: "CRITICAL: Respond with TEXT ONLY. Do NOT call any tools. - Do NOT use Read, Bash, Grep, Glob, Edit, Write, or ANY other tool. - You already have all the context you need in the conversation above. - Tool calls will be REJECTED and will waste your only turn — you will fail the task. - Your entire response must be plain text: an <analysis> block followed by a <summary> block."

7. Pending Tasks:
   - None. The user's exploration request has been completed. The user has now requested a summary of the conversation.

8. Current Work:
   - At the time of the summary request, I had just finished exploring all the requested files and providing detailed findings about the Duality Pole Studio codebase. The exploration identified that SeasonEnrolmentsPage.jsx does not exist, but AdminSeasonDetail.jsx and AdminSeasonOverview.jsx do exist. The "View Roster" button was found in AdminSeasonOverview.jsx linking to the attendance page. API calls for payments and enrolments were enumerated from the api/index.js file. Payment methods supported are: Card (Stripe), Cash, Bank Transfer, Account Credit, and Other. The main payment creation endpoint is POST `/api/payments/`.

9. Optional Next Step:
   - None. The user's request has been fulfilled. The conversation has concluded with a request for a summary, which is now being provided. No additional tasks are pending or requested.

file.content:
class Payment(models.Model):
    class PaymentType(models.TextChoices):
        PAYMENT = 'payment', 'Payment'
        CHARGE = 'charge', 'Charge'
        REFUND = 'refund', 'Refund'
        CREDIT = 'credit', 'Credit'
        NO_SHOW_FEE = 'no_show_fee', 'No-show Fee'

    class PaymentMethod(models.TextChoices):
        CARD = 'card', 'Credit / Debit Card'
        CASH = 'cash', 'Cash'
        BANK = 'bank_transfer', 'Bank Transfer'
        ACCOUNT = 'account_credit', 'Account Credit'
        OTHER = 'other', 'Other'

    student = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='payments')
    payment_type = models.CharField(max_length=15, choices=PaymentType.choices)
    amount = models.DecimalField(max_digits=8, decimal_places=2)
    description = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    reference = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True)
    payment_method = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=PaymentMethod.choices, blank=True, default='')
    stripe_payment_intent_id = models.CharField(max_length=100, blank=True, default='')
    created_by = models.ForeignKey(
        User, on_delete=models.SET_NULL, null=True, related_name='payments_recorded'
    )
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    admin_notes = models.TextField(blank=True)

    # Cash promise tracking

content.thinking:
Now I have enough information to implement all the requested features. Let me plan what I need to do:

**1. Fix back button** - The `← ` button in AdminClassEnrolments uses `navigate(-1)` which should work. The user says it navigates to Class Styles. This might be because the timetable's "See Enrolments" button uses `navigate()` imperatively. The issue could be that the back navigation history stack is getting messed up. Let me see what the Timetable's "See Enrolments" does.

Actually, looking at the code in AdminTimetable.jsx line 686: `navigate(\`/admin/classes/${s.id}/season-enrolments\`)` - this should push to history so `navigate(-1)` should go back to timetable.


But I'm realizing the navigation flow might be different than I thought—the user could be accessing the enrolments page from the Season page instead, which has different navigation patterns, so the back button behavior would depend on which page they came from. if they navigated there from a link or the AdminTimetable's "See Enrolments" button. The real problem is that `navigate(-1)` relies on browser history, which breaks if the page is refreshed or loaded directly. A more reliable approach would be to pass the previous page location through state when navigating, so the back button always knows where to go.

For the AdminClassEnrolments page, I need to add several new features: action buttons like Transfer, Pay, and Chat on the Enrolled tab, a new Cancelled tab showing cancelled enrolments with reinstate options and refund details, and an "Add to class" button on the Waitlist tab that can override capacity limits. There's also an Email class feature to implement.

For AdminSeasonOverview, I'm changing "View Roster" to "See Enrolments" which links to the season enrolments page instead of attendance, adding the ability to create new classes from the Edit Season area, splitting the class list by day, and offering a calendar view option.

I'll start by tackling AdminClassEnrolments systematically—fixing the back button navigation, adding the new buttons and tabs, then moving on to AdminSeasonOverview with its layout and linking changes.

For the enrolments page itself, I need to add an Action column with Transfer, Pay, Chat, and Cancel buttons to the Enrolled tab, a "Add to class" button for each Waitlist entry, and a reinstate option in the Cancelled tab. The "Add Student" and "Pay" buttons will open a comprehensive payment modal that handles cost overrides with reasons, multiple payment methods including cash, bank transfer, account credit with balance display, saved card charging, payment plans, fee waivers, and custom amounts.

I'm checking the backend to see what endpoints are available—there's a POST endpoint for creating payment records, one for charging saved Stripe cards, and one for creating enrolments, so I'll need to understand how the enrolment detail endpoint works to complete the flow.

Looking at the enrolment endpoints, I can PATCH the status field to cancel or reinstate enrolments, and there's a `promote-waitlist` endpoint to move waitlist entries to active status. Now I'm planning out the full rewrite of the AdminClassEnrolments component and what changes are needed in AdminSeasonOverview.

The component will need state for managing the current tab, modals for adding students, handling emails, recording payments, and managing cancellations and reinstatements. On the backend side, the `season-enrolments` endpoint needs to include cancelled enrolments in its response, and I'll use PATCH requests to update enrolment status, POST to the payments endpoint to record payments, and GET the student balance endpoint to check payment status.

Looking at the waitlist promotion endpoint, it seems to promote a waitlist entry to an active enrolment, though I should verify whether it respects class capacity. For now I'll implement the frontend and handle whatever response comes back. The main implementation work involves updating the backend view to return cancelled enrolments, then rewriting the AdminClassEnrolments component with all the new modal and state management logic, followed by updating AdminSeasonOverview to coordinate everything.

transfers_out = (
            ClassChangeRequest.objects
            .filter(current_enrolment__class_session=session, request_type='transfer')
            .exclude(requested_session=session)
            .select_related('student', 'requested_session')
        )

        enrolled_data = []
        for e in enrolled_qs:
            count = season_enrolment_count(e.student)
            enrolled_data.append({
                'id': e.id,
                'student_id': e.student_id,
                'student_name': e.student.display_name,
                'student_level': e.student.level,
                'enrolled_date': e.enrolled_date,
                'is_first_visit': e.is_first_visit,
                'level_override': e.level_override,
                'flag_dismissed': e.flag_dismissed,
                'is_new_to_duality': is_new_to_duality(e),
                'season_enrolment_count': count,
                'incremental_price': incremental_price(count),
                'notes': e.notes,
            })

        waitlist_data = []
        for e in waitlist_qs:
            waitlist_data.append({
                'id': e.id,
                'student_id': e.student_id,
                'student_name': e.student.display_name,
                'student_level': e.student.level,
                'waitlist_position': e.waitlist_position,
                'enrolled_date': e.enrolled_date,
            })

        transfers_data = []
        for t in transfers_in:
            from_class = None
            if t.current_enrolment and t.current_enrolment.class_session:
                from_class = t.current_enrolment.class_session.name
            transfers_data.append({
                'id': t.id,
                'direction': 'in',
                'student_id': t.student_id,
                'student_name': t.student.display_name,
                'from_class': from_class,
                'to_class': session.name,
                'status': t.status,
                'created_at': t.created_at,
                'notes': t.notes,
            })
        for t in transfers_out:
            transfers_data.append({
                'id': t.id,
                'direction': 'out',
                'student_id': t.student_id,
                'student_name': t.student.display_name,
                'from_class': session.name,
                'to_class': t.requested_session.name if t.requested_session else None,
                'status': t.status,
                'created_at': t.created_at,
                'notes': t.notes,
            })
        transfers_data.sort(key=lambda x: x['created_at'], reverse=True)

        return Response({
            'session': {
                'id': session.id,
                'name': session.name,
                'level': session.level,
                'day_of_week': session.get_day_of_week_display(),
                'start_time': str(session.start_time)[:5] if session.start_time else None,
                'instructor': session.instructor.display_name if session.instructor else None,
                'studio': session.studio.name if session.studio else None,
                'season': season.name if season else None,
                'capacity': session.capacity,
                'enrolled_count': session.enrolled_count,
            },
            'enrolled': enrolled_data,

**Frontend changes:**

**AdminClassEnrolments.jsx - major rewrite:**


I'm restructuring the component with four tabs for Enrolled, Waitlist, Cancelled, and Transfers. The top action bar will have buttons to add students and email the class. The Enrolled tab displays student names with links, their level, enrollment date, class count, price paid, and various flags, with actions to pay, transfer, chat, or cancel. The Waitlist tab shows position, student info, and level with an option to add students to the class with capacity validation. The Cancelled tab lists students with their cancellation date, reason from notes, refund information, and a reinstate action.

I need to build six modals: one for adding new students with payment, another for recording payments on existing enrollments, a cancellation modal with credit/refund options, an email modal, a reinstate confirmation modal, and a modal to add waitlisted students to the class. I'm also updating the AdminSeasonOverview component to change "View Roster" to "See Enrolments" linking to the new enrolments page, grouping sessions by day of the week, and adding an "Add Class" button that opens a modal to create a new class session for the season.

For adding a new class to the season, I'll keep it simple by navigating to the class creation page with the season ID pre-filled in the query string. Now I'm going to implement all of this systematically, starting with updating the backend to include cancelled enrollments in the season-enrolments endpoint, then rewriting the AdminClassEnrolments component and updating AdminSeasonOverview. I'm also thinking through the payment flow and what payment methods need to be available. I need to figure out how to fetch a student's saved Stripe cards when an admin is processing a payment on their behalf—the existing endpoint probably only returns the current user's methods, so I might need a dedicated admin endpoint for this. For now, I'll focus on getting the cash and bank transfer flows working in the payment modal, then circle back to the card charging logic.

Writing component code... loading, tab, addStudentModal, emailModal, payModal, cancelModal, reinstateModal, addFromWaitlistModal. After the API call, I need to fetch cancelled enrolments separately—actually, I should update the backend to return those alongside the active ones. The interface will have four tabs: Enrolled, Waitlist, Cancelled, and Transfers. The Enrolled table shows student, level, enrolment date, class number, price, flags, and actions like Pay, Transfer, Chat, and Cancel. Waitlist displays the join order, student info, and an option to add to class. Cancelled lists the student, cancellation date, reason, and credit/refund status with a Reinstate action. I'll need modals for adding students with enrolment type and payment, processing payments, handling cancellations with refund options, emailing the class, confirming reinstatements, and adding from the waitlist. Now I'm updating the backend to include cancelled enrolments in the response...

Writing backend query... Writing modal components...

I'm also thinking through the transfer flow—it'll need to create a ClassChangeRequest by showing a modal to select the target class. For the chat feature, I'll link to the student messaging interface. Now I need to figure out which backend endpoints are missing and what I'll need to build out.

class AdminPromoteSeasonWaitlistView(APIView):
    """Admin: promote a waitlisted season enrolment to active, with optional capacity override."""
    permission_classes = [IsAdminOrInstructor]

    def post(self, request, pk):
        enrolment = get_object_or_404(Enrolment, pk=pk, status='waitlisted')
        override = request.data.get('override_capacity', False)

        session = enrolment.class_session
        active_count = Enrolment.objects.filter(class_session=session, status='active').count()
        capacity = getattr(session, 'capacity', None)

        if not override and capacity and active_count >= capacity:
            return Response({
                'detail': 'Class is at capacity.',
                'current': active_count,
                'capacity': capacity,
                'requires_override': True,
            }, status=status.HTTP_409_CONFLICT)

        enrolment.status = 'active'
        enrolment.waitlist_offered_at = None
        enrolment.waitlist_expires_at = None
        enrolment.waitlist_urgent = False
        enrolment.waitlist_position = None
        enrolment.save(update_fields=['status', 'waitlist_offered_at', 'waitlist_expires_at', 'waitlist_urgent', 'waitlist_position'])

        from apps.users.models import Notification
        Notification.objects.create(
            recipient=enrolment.student,
            title=f"You're in! — {session.name}",
            body=f"A spot opened up in {session.name} and you've been promoted from the waitlist. You're now enrolled!",
            notification_type='success',
        )

        return Response(EnrolmentSerializer(enrolment).data)


class AdminSendSeasonWaitlistOfferView(APIView):
    """Admin: manually send (or resend) a waitlist offer with optional custom expiry."""
    permission_classes = [IsAdminOrInstructor]

    def post(self, request, pk):
        from datetime import timedelta
        from apps.users.models import Notification

+    permission_classes = [IsAdminOrInstructor]

+    def get(self, request, pk):
+        from apps.enrolments.models import Enrolment, ClassChangeRequest
+
+        try:
+            session = ClassSession.objects.select_related('instructor', 'studio', 'season').get(pk=pk)
+        except ClassSession.DoesNotExist:
+            return Response({'detail': 'Not found.'}, status=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND)
+
+        season = session.season
+        season_id = season.id if season else None
+
+        PRICE_TIERS = [0, 270, 440, 580, 700, 800, 900]
+
+        def incremental_price(count):
+            if not count or count <= 0:
+                return None
+            idx = min(count, len(PRICE_TIERS) - 1)
+            return PRICE_TIERS[idx] - PRICE_TIERS[idx - 1]
+
+        def season_enrolment_count(student):
+            if not season_id:
+                return None
+            session_ids = ClassSession.objects.filter(season_id=season_id).values_list('id', flat=True)
+            return Enrolment.objects.filter(
+                student=student,
+                class_session_id__in=session_ids,
+                status='active',
+                enrolment_type='course',

+
+        def is_new_to_duality(enrolment):
+            return not Enrolment.objects.filter(
+                student=enrolment.student,
+                enrolled_date__lt=enrolment.enrolled_date,
+                enrolment_type='course',
+            ).exists()
+
+        enrolled_qs = (
+            Enrolment.objects
+            .filter(class_session=session, status='active', enrolment_type='course')
+            .select_related('student')
+            .order_by('enrolled_date')
+        )
+
+        waitlist_qs = (
+            Enrolment.objects
+            .filter(class_session=session, status='waitlisted')
+            .select_related('student')
+            .order_by('waitlist_position', 'enrolled_date')
+        )
+
+        transfers_in = (
+            ClassChangeRequest.objects
+            .filter(requested_session=session, request_type='transfer')
+            .select_related('student', 'current_enrolment__class_session')
+        )
+
+        transfers_out = (
+            ClassChangeRequest.objects
+            .filter(current_enrolment__class_session=session, request_type='transfer')
+            .exclude(requested_session=session)
+            .select_related('student', 'requested_session')
+        )
+
+        enrolled_data = []
+        for e in enrolled_qs:
+            count = season_enrolment_count(e.student)
+            enrolled_data.append({
+                'id': e.id,
+                'student_id': e.student_id,
+                'student_name': e.student.display_name,
+                'student_level': e.student.level,
+                'enrolled_date': e.enrolled_date,
+                'is_first_visit': e.is_first_visit,
+                'level_override': e.level_override,
+                'flag_dismissed': e.flag_dismissed,
+                'is_new_to_duality': is_new_to_duality(e),
+                'season_enrolment_count': count,
+                'incremental_price': incremental_price(count),
+                'notes': e.notes,
+            })
+
+        waitlist_data = []
+        for e in waitlist_qs:
+            waitlist_data.append({
+                'id': e.id,
+                'student_id': e.student_id,
+                'student_name': e.student.display_name,
+                'student_level': e.student.level,
+                'waitlist_position': e.waitlist_position,
+                'enrolled_date': e.enrolled_date,
+            })
+
+        transfers_data = []
+        for t in transfers_in:
+            from_class = None
+            if t.current_enrolment and t.current_enrolment.class_session:
+                from_class = t.current_enrolment.class_session.name
+            transfers_data.append({
+                'id': t.id,
+                'direction': 'in',
+                'student_id': t.student_id,
+                'student_name': t.student.display_name,
+                'from_class': from_class,
+                'to_class': session.name,
+                'status': t.status,
+                'created_at': t.created_at,
+                'notes': t.notes,
+            })
+        for t in transfers_out:
+            transfers_data.append({
+                'id': t.id,
+                'direction': 'out',
+                'student_id': t.student_id,
+                'student_name': t.student.display_name,
+                'from_class': session.name,
+                'to_class': t.requested_session.name if t.requested_session else None,
+                'status': t.status,
+                'created_at': t.created_at,
+                'notes': t.notes,
+            })
+        transfers_data.sort(key=lambda x: x['created_at'], reverse=True)
+
+        cancelled_qs = (
+            Enrolment.objects
+            .filter(class_session=session, status='cancelled')

+        else:
+            user_prompt = (
+                f'Write a short class description for "{name}" at Duality Pole Studio.\n'
+                + (f'Level: {level}\n' if level else '')

+            )

+        BRAND_VOICE_SYSTEM = """You write class copy for Duality Pole Studio — a boutique pole dancing studio on Gadigal Land in Surry Hills, Sydney.
+
+BRAND VOICE:
+- Short, punchy sentences. Fragments are fine. Even encouraged.
+- Direct. Confident. Warm but never gushing.
+- Playful — dry humour lands well here. Don't force it though.
+- Unapologetically comfortable with words like sensual, sexy, tease, flirty. Don't shy away from them.
+- Inclusive — every body, every background, every level.
+- Never corporate. Never a gym. Never over-explained.
+- No hashtags. No emojis. No exclamation marks unless it's genuinely earned.
+
+REAL EXAMPLES OF OUR CLASS DESCRIPTIONS (match this tone exactly):
+
+Level 1: "Your pole era starts here. Learn spins, grips, floorwork to build strength and tease out your sensual side. You'll also learn how to put it all together and dance a full routine. Perfect if you've never touched a pole before (and no, drunk on Cityrail doesn't count)."
+
+Level 2: "Can you execute all your tricks from Level 1? Wow, you're amazing! Time to climb higher, invert for the first time (yes, that's upside down — you're ready)."
+
+Level 3: "Getting spicy. You'll build on your inversions, add climbing, and your routine will start to feel more like a real performance."
+
+Floor Virgin: "Get low. This one's all about floorwork — crawls, rolls, transitions, and moves that make the ground your dance floor."
+
+Strip Virgin: "Flirty, fun, and unapologetically sexy. Learn the art of the tease through chair work, floor play, and a whole lot of attitude."
+
+Dance Virgin: "Your first taste of pure movement. No pole required (well, there's one nearby, but you won't need it). Floor-based, fun, and full of personality."
+
+Kiki: "Conditioning disguised as fun. Kiki is a conditioning class that builds the strength, stability, and flexibility you need to level up your pole skills — through movement that doesn't feel like a workout (but absolutely is)."
+
+Unravel: "Stretching and flexibility for pole dancers. Designed to open you up safely and progressively so you can access the shapes you've been dreaming about."
+
+Invert Tech: "Technical work on inversions — breaking them down, building strength, and refining entry and exit."
+
+REAL EXAMPLES OF OUR FIRST-TIMER INFO (what to wear / bring / expect):
+
+What to wear: "Shorts are essential. Skin contact with the pole is part of how grip works, so you need exposed legs and ideally a sports crop or fitted top. You can change at the studio — no need to rock up in your pole gear."
+
+What to bring: "Your water bottle, and a good attitude. Everything else you'll figure out as you go. Grip aids are available at the studio if you need them."
+
+What to expect: "Show up on time. Your instructor will take you through a warm-up before getting into the class content. Put your phone away — you can exist without Instagram for 50 minutes. If you're going to be late, let your instructor know in advance."
+
+RULES:
+- Class descriptions: 2–3 sentences max. What it is, what you do, who it's for.
+- First-timer body: short practical points. What to wear, what to bring, what to expect. Warm and real, not corporate safety briefing.
+- Never write "journey". Never write "transform". Never write "amazing community" or "safe space". Show it, don't say it.
+- Never mention weight loss, toning, or burning calories as reasons to do pole. Strength, fitness, and sweating are fine — "will have you sweating more than hot yoga" is perfect. The goal is skill, artistry, and feeling good, not changing how your body looks.
+- Never say anything that implies a student should want to change how their body looks.

input.body:
## Summary

### Season Enrolments page (`/admin/classes/:id/season-enrolments`)
- **Add Student** — search students, choose enrolment type, set amount (editable + reason field), choose payment method (Cash / Bank Transfer / Account Credit with live balance / Charge Saved Card / Waive Fee), add notes
- **Email Class** — compose and send email to all enrolled students
- **Actions per student**: Pay (opens full payment flow), Transfer (search target class), Chat (links to student messages), Cancel (with credit options: no credit / account credit with amount / refund to card)
- **Cancelled tab** — shows cancelled enrolments with cancel notes/reason, Reinstate button (one-click)
- **Waitlist → Add to Class** — promotes from waitlist; if at capacity shows override warning (casual bookings still limited to original capacity)
- **Back button** — uses browser history; falls back to `/admin/timetable` if navigated to directly
- Tabs: **Enrolled | Waitlist | Cancelled | Transfers**

### Season Overview page (`/admin/seasons/:id`)
- "View Roster" → **"See Enrolments"** (now correctly links to season-enrolments page)
- Added **"Attendance"** as a separate quick link
- Class list **grouped by day** (Monday, Tuesday… Sunday)
- **+ Add Class** button (links to Timetable with season context)

### Backend
- `ClassSeasonEnrolmentsView` now includes `cancelled` array in response (student name, level, phone, cancel date, notes, type)

## Test plan
- [ ] Season Overview: classes appear grouped by day
- [ ] "See Enrolments" goes to season-enrolments page; "Attendance" goes to attendance register
- [ ] Season Enrolments: Pay modal opens pre-filled with incremental price; amount editable; payment records on submit
- [ ] Cancel modal: credit option adds account credit; notes saved on enrolment
- [ ] Cancelled tab shows cancelled enrolments; Reinstate moves back to enrolled
- [ ] Waitlist "Add to Class" works; shows override warning when full
- [ ] Transfer modal: search classes, submit creates change request
- [ ] Email Class sends to enrolled students
- [ ] Add Student: search + select + set amount + payment method + add works end-to-end
- [ ] Back button from season-enrolments goes to previous page (timetable or season overview)

PR #54 is up: https://github.com/allym-4/test/pull/54

Here's what's changed:

**Season Enrolments page** — now a full management hub:
- **+ Add Student** with multi-step payment flow (amount editable with reason field, methods: Cash / Bank Transfer / Account Credit / Charge Saved Card / Waive)
- **Email Class** button to message all enrolled students
- **Per-student actions**: Pay (pre-filled with their incremental price), Transfer (search + select target class), Chat (→ student messages), Cancel (no credit / account credit / Stripe refund)
- **Cancelled tab** — shows reason/notes, one-click Reinstate
- **Waitlist "Add to Class"** — promotes them; if full shows a capacity override warning with the note that casuals stay at original capacity
- **Back button** fixed — uses browser history, falls back to Timetable

**Season Overview page:**
- "View Roster" → **"See Enrolments"** (correct destination now)
- Classes split **by day** (Monday group, Tuesday group, etc.)
- **+ Add Class** button

The branch has commits that were squash-merged into main. So the regular rebase finds conflicts because the changes are already in main but in a different form. 

The simplest approach here is to:
1. Create a new branch from main
2. Cherry-pick only the changes that aren't already in main (the last 2-3 commits)
3. Force push to the PR branch

- `b26b0c2` - Timetable colors (already in main from before)

So PR #54 is really just PR #53 plus the new Season Enrolments commit. Since PR #53 was squash-merged, git sees those commits as different even though they're already in main. The fix is straightforward: create a fresh branch from main and cherry-pick only the latest commit that actually adds new functionality.

- session-start-hook: Creating and developing startup hooks for Claude Code on the web. Use when the user wants to set up a repository for Claude Code on the web, create a SessionStart hook to ensure their project can run tests and linters during web sessions.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

content.thinking:
The user has several requests. Let me break them down:

1. **Season Enrolments page - "Cancel Class" button**: Should have notify students/cancel quietly options, then refund/credit options per person or bulk (like other class cancellations do)

2. **Attendance Register navigation**: When clicking "Attendance Register" from Season Enrolments page, it should go to the specific class occurrence (the upcoming classes single occurrence date view - screenshot 2)

3. **Attendance page (screenshot 2) - Week selector**: A week dropdown showing "Week X / 8" for the season, with tiles below the title box (above the action buttons) showing each week with date. Single classes don't need this.


4. **Keep "Season Enrolments" breadcrumb/button** on the attendance page for all weeks

5. **"Email Class" → "Contact Class"**: With options for email, push notification, and a pop-up/modal that students must acknowledge, with customisable buttons/CTAs

6. **Edit Class back button**: Should go back to the appropriate season, not Class Styles

7. **Edit Class auto-populate from Class Styles**: When adding Dance Tech to Season 3, it should auto-populate from the Class Styles setup

8. **Categories management**: "Manage Categories" or "+Add new" option in the category dropdown

9. **Seasons > Edit Season - clarify "Close Bookings" vs "Disable Enrolments"**: The user is asking what the distinction is between these two actions

. "Close Bookings" prevents new bookings for the season's classes, while "Disable Enrolments" acts as a global kill-switch to prevent any enrollments entirely. I need to check the backend to confirm how these toggles actually work.

Now I'm prioritizing the implementation: adding a cancel class button to the Season Enrolments page with a modal for notification and refund options, fixing the attendance register link to navigate to specific weeks, and adding a week selector dropdown on the attendance page.

There's quite a bit here to tackle, so I'll work through each feature systematically and see what's realistically achievable given the current codebase structure.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture. "Class Styles" in the nav is `/admin/classes` which is the `AdminClasses` page. When you press "Edit Class" from within the AttendancePage or Season Enrolments page, it goes to `/admin/classes/:id`, and when you save or cancel there, it navigates to `/admin/classes` (the Class Styles list).

The issue is:
1. The back button/cancel in AdminClassDetail always goes to `/admin/classes` (Class Styles), not to the season or the attendance page.
2. The user wants it to go back to the season when coming from there.


3. I can fix this by passing the referrer through location state and using that as the fallback destination, or simply using the browser's back button. For the auto-populate feature, I'd need to implement a "Copy from" mechanism that detects matching class names and pre-fills the form accordingly.

Actually, I'm realizing there's no separate ClassStyle model—the "Class Styles" page just displays all ClassSession objects. So when a user wants to auto-populate, they're looking to copy settings from an existing class with the same name in a previous season. There's already a copy season feature in place, so I could leverage that pattern to let users select a source class and copy its properties when creating a new one.

For the Season Enrolments page, I need to check if the CancelClassModal from AttendancePage can be reused here, though I'll need to figure out how to pass the occurrence data properly. object for this. In the Season Enrolments context, there's no specific occurrence selected.
- Actually the "Cancel Class" for season enrolments is more about "cancelling a class occurrence for a specific week" + managing refunds/credits for enrolled students
- The cancel class action should:
  1. Ask: notify students (yes/no) or cancel quietly
  2. Show list of enrolled students with credit/refund options per person or bulk
  3. Execute the cancellation

This is more complex than the attendance page cancel. The AttendancePage has an occurrence selected (the current one), but the Season Enrolments page doesn't have an occurrence context.

Wait - the user says "like other classes do". Looking at AttendancePage's CancelClassModal at line 269, it cancels a specific occurrence. But the user wants this on the Season Enrolments page too.

I think the user means: the Cancel Class on the Season Enrolments page should work similarly to what's already on the Attendance page, but with the added complexity of selecting which occurrence to cancel since there's no current week context here. I'd need to let users pick a specific week or cancel all remaining weeks, then handle the student notifications and per-student refund or credit options. This mirrors what happens elsewhere in the system but adapted for the season view where multiple occurrences are available. So the navigation is already correct - the attendance page loads today's occurrence automatically. What I need to add is a week selector on that page itself. I'll need to load all occurrences for the session, calculate their week numbers based on the season's start date, and display them as tiles so the user can switch between weeks. The selector should appear under the title and above the action buttons, and I can derive each week's occurrence by adding multiples of 7 days to the season start date.

For the contact feature, I'm replacing "Email Attending" with a "Contact Class" button that opens a modal with multiple options - email, push notification, or a pop-up acknowledgment. The pop-up type is more involved since it requires a backend endpoint to create a blocking notification that students see when they open the app, so I'll build out the frontend UI for now and note that the pop-up functionality may need additional backend support.

For the back button on the edit class page, I should check the browser history to determine whether to return to the classes list or stay within the current navigation context, rather than always routing back to the main classes page.

When creating a new class, I want to add a "Copy from" dropdown that lets users select an existing class and automatically populate fields like the description, first-timer messaging, and catchup settings.

For category management, I'm adding a "manage categories" link and a quick "Add new" option directly in the category dropdown within the class detail view.

Now I'm examining the existing AttendancePage to understand how the week selector works...

message.content:
Implement these changes across multiple frontend files in /home/user/test/frontend/src. Read each file before editing it.

## 1. AttendancePage.jsx (`/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/AttendancePage.jsx`)

### Week selector (tiles + dropdown)
The page already loads all occurrences: `const occs = occRes.data.results || []` and sets `occurrence` state.

The session serializer already provides `session.season_start_date` and `session.season_end_date`.

Add a week selector UI that:
- Only shows if `session.season_start_date` is set AND `occs.length > 1`  
- Sits between the class info card and the action buttons (currently at line ~522)
- Shows tiles: `| Week 2 | | Jun 29 |` styled as horizontal scrolling tiles, where clicking switches the current occurrence
- Each tile shows the week number (calculated as `Math.ceil((new Date(occ.date) - new Date(session.season_start_date)) / (7*24*60*60*1000)) + 1`) and the date formatted as "29 Jun"
- Active tile has lime border/accent, cancelled occurrences get a red tint
- Total weeks = `Math.round((new Date(session.season_end_date) - new Date(session.season_start_date)) / (7*24*60*60*1000))`
- Also add a compact `<select>` dropdown saying "Week X / Y" that can switch the occurrence — place it as a small dropdown to the right of the class date in the info card

attachment.content:
- session-start-hook: Creating and developing startup hooks for Claude Code on the web. Use when the user wants to set up a repository for Claude Code on the web, create a SessionStart hook to ensure their project can run tests and linters during web sessions.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

toolUseResult.description: Implement multiple improvements across attendance, season enrolments, and edit class pages
toolUseResult.prompt:
Implement these changes across multiple frontend files in /home/user/test/frontend/src. Read each file before editing it.

## 1. AttendancePage.jsx (`/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/AttendancePage.jsx`)

### Week selector (tiles + dropdown)
The page already loads all occurrences: `const occs = occRes.data.results || []` and sets `occurrence` state.

The session serializer already provides `session.season_start_date` and `session.season_end_date`.

Add a week selector UI that:
- Only shows if `session.season_start_date` is set AND `occs.length > 1`  
- Sits between the class info card and the action buttons (currently at line ~522)
- Shows tiles: `| Week 2 | | Jun 29 |` styled as horizontal scrolling tiles, where clicking switches the current occurrence
- Each tile shows the week number (calculated as `Math.ceil((new Date(occ.date) - new Date(session.season_start_date)) / (7*24*60*60*1000)) + 1`) and the date formatted as "29 Jun"
- Active tile has lime border/accent, cancelled occurrences get a red tint
- Total weeks = `Math.round((new Date(session.season_end_date) - new Date(session.season_start_date)) / (7*24*60*60*1000))`
- Also add a compact `<select>` dropdown saying "Week X / Y" that can switch the occurrence — place it as a small dropdown to the right of the class date in the info card

When a tile/dropdown is clicked, switching the occurrence requires also reloading the attendance data for that occurrence. Currently attendance is loaded in the initial `load()` function. Create a separate `loadOccurrence(occ)` function that:
- Sets `occurrence` to the new occ
- Fetches attendance for that occurrence
- Resets `register`, `notes`, `noteTags` from the new data
- Sets `saved = false`

Also need to store `occs` in state: add `const [occurrences, setOccurrences] = useState([])` and populate it in load(). Pass it to the week selector.

### Replace "Email Attending" with "Contact Class"
Replace the "Email Attending" button with "Contact Class". Instead of opening `emailModal`, open a new `contactModal`. Create a `ContactClassModal` component with:
- Three tabs: "Email", "Push Notification", "Pop-up Alert"
- **Email tab** (default): Same as current EmailModal — subject, body fields; sends to `POST /api/classes/sessions/${id}/email/` with `{subject, body, recipients: 'enrolled'}`
- **Push Notification tab**: Message field (shorter, ~160 chars); sends to `POST /api/classes/sessions/${id}/notify/` with `{message, type: 'push'}`; if endpoint 404s, show "Push notifications aren't enabled yet on this server"
- **Pop-up Alert tab**: Title + Message + optional CTA button label + CTA URL; sends to `POST /api/classes/sessions/${id}/notify/` with `{title, message, cta_label, cta_url, type: 'popup'}`; same 404 fallback message

### Keep existing Cancel Class modal — don't change it

## 2. AdminClassEnrolments.jsx (`/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminClassEnrolments.jsx`)

### Add "Cancel Class" button to the action bar
In the header action buttons (currently: `+ Add Student`, `Email Class`, `Edit Class`, `Attendance Register`), replace "Email Class" with "Contact Class" (same pattern as above — same ContactClassModal reused, passing `sessionId` and `enrolled`) and add a "Cancel Class" button (red text) that opens a `CancelClassModal`.

The CancelClassModal for this page should:
- Ask: notify enrolled students (checkbox, default true) 
- Option: "Cancel quietly" (no notification sent) vs "Notify students"
- If notifying: show a text field for reason/message
- Show a "Refund / Credit" section with:
  - Radio: No credit | Issue account credit to all | Review per-student
  - If "Issue account credit to all": show an amount field (default: the incremental price / session price)
  - If "Review per-student": show a list of enrolled students with individual credit amount fields (pre-filled)
- On confirm: 
  - Get today's occurrence via `GET /api/classes/occurrences/?session=:id` and find today's or next upcoming date  
  - PATCH the occurrence status to 'cancelled': `PATCH /api/classes/occurrences/:occId/`
  - If notifying: `POST /api/classes/sessions/:id/email/` with subject/body
  - If issuing credits: for each student, `POST /api/payments/` with credit record
- This is the same cancel flow used in the AttendancePage's CancelClassModal, but enhanced with credit options.

For this page, keep the existing "Email Class" behaviour (we're keeping that state/modal) — just rename the button label to "Contact Class" and wire to ContactClassModal.

Actually, re-reading: the user said replace "Email Class" with "Contact Class" AND add "Cancel Class". So:
- Old: `[+ Add Student] [Email Class] [Edit Class] [Attendance Register]`
- New: `[+ Add Student] [Contact Class] [Edit Class] [Attendance Register] [Cancel Class]`

## 3. AdminClassDetail.jsx (`/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminClassDetail.jsx`)

### Fix back button
Currently both the `Cancel` button and `navigate('/admin/classes')` after save go to `/admin/classes`. 

Change behaviour:
- Use `useLocation()` from react-router
- Add `const backTo = location.state?.from || '/admin/classes'` 
- Change all `navigate('/admin/classes')` calls to `navigate(backTo)` or `navigate(location.state?.from || -1)` as fallback
- For the Cancel button in the header: use `navigate(location.key !== 'default' ? -1 : '/admin/classes')`
- For the Cancel button at the bottom: same
- After successful save: same back destination

### Auto-populate from existing class when creating new
In the create new flow (when `isNew === true`), add a "Copy settings from class" UI:
- Add state: `const [copyFromId, setCopyFromId] = useState('')`
- Before the form fields (near the top of the form), add a section: "Copy settings from" — a searchable select that lists all available sessions (already loaded in `allSessions`)
- When a session is selected from this dropdown: populate the form fields from that session's data: name, description, first_timer_headline, first_timer_body, catchup_cutoff_weeks, level, session_type, duration_minutes, capacity, category, prerequisites, skill_level
- Don't copy: season, instructor, studio, day_of_week, start_time (these should be set fresh)
- The select should show: "{session.name} — {season_name}" for each option, sorted by name then by season_start_date desc
- Style it like other inputs in the form

### Add "Manage Categories" link next to category dropdown
Currently the category dropdown is at line ~444. Next to the "Category" label, add a small link `<Link to="/admin/categories" style={{ fontSize: 11, color: 'var(--lime)', textDecoration: 'none' }}>Manage categories →</Link>` — same pattern as the existing "Manage skill lists →" link next to Skill Level.

Change the label+link layout to match: use the same `display: flex, alignItems: center, justifyContent: space-between` wrapper for the label row, then the select below.

## Implementation notes

- Read each file fully before editing
- The ContactClassModal is a new component — create it in each file that needs it (AttendancePage.jsx and AdminClassEnrolments.jsx can have their own copy, or if you prefer DRY, put it in a shared file but the user hasn't set that up, so just duplicate the component with the right state wiring per file)
- Keep all existing functionality intact
- Dark theme: background #111/#1a1a1a, accent var(--lime) = #ccff00, borders var(--border) = #222
- After changes, commit and push to `claude/resume-session-CqPoE`

Start by reading the three files, then implement all changes.

today.toDateString())) { 45 thisYear = new Date(today.getFullYear() + 1, dob.getMonth(), dob.getDate()) 46 } 47 const diff = Math.round((thisYear - today) / 86400000) 48 if (diff === 0) badges.push({ label: 'Birthday today 🎂', color: '#ffaa00', bg: 'rgba(255,170,0,0.1)', border: 'rgba(255,170,0,0.35)' }) 49 else if (diff === 1) badges.push({ label: 'Birthday tomorrow 🎂', color: '#ffaa00', bg: 'rgba(255,170,0,0.08)', border: 'rgba(255,170,0,0.25)' }) 50 else if (diff > 1 && diff <= 7) badges.push({ label: `Birthday in ${diff} days 🎂`, color: '#ffaa00', bg: 'rgba(255,170,0,0.06)', border: content.thinking:
While the agent works on those frontend changes, let me answer the user's question about "Close Bookings" vs "Disable Enrolments" and plan what else I can tell them.

I found in the backend model:
```python
bookings_open = models.BooleanField(default=False)
# go_live_at: if set, bookings auto-open at this UTC datetime; overrides bookings_open=False
# bookings_enabled: admin kill-switch for season enrolments (does not affect casuals)


bookings_enabled = models.BooleanField(default=True)
```

So the distinction is clear: `bookings_open` is your day-to-day toggle for when the season's enrollment window is active, while `bookings_enabled` is an admin-level kill-switch that completely disables season enrolments (though it leaves casual bookings untouched) — useful when you're still setting up or troubleshooting.

content.text:
The agent is implementing all the frontend changes. While that runs, let me answer your question about Close Bookings vs Disable Enrolments:

---

**Close Bookings** (`bookings_open` toggle)
Closes the booking window — students can no longer see or make new season enrolments. This is the standard timing control: you open it at 8am when the season goes live, close it once you've hit your numbers or the season starts. It can also be scheduled to auto-open at a specific datetime.

**Disable Enrolments** (`bookings_enabled` kill-switch)
A harder admin override that disables season enrolments entirely — even if bookings are "open", no one can enrol. Importantly, it **does not affect casual bookings**, so students can still drop in. You'd use this if the season setup isn't complete yet, or if you need to pause enrolments while you sort something out without fully "closing" the season.

Think of it as:
- **Disable Enrolments** = "the season isn't ready / enrolments are paused" (back-office switch)  
- **Close Bookings** = "the enrolment window has closed" (normal lifecycle toggle)

---

The agent is working on: week selector on attendance, Contact Class modal, Cancel Class with per-student credit options on Season Enrolments, back button fix on Edit Class, copy-from-existing on new class, and Manage Categories link. I'll share a summary once it's done.

content.text:
All changes have been implemented, built successfully, committed, and pushed. Here's a summary of what was done:

---

**AttendancePage.jsx (`/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/AttendancePage.jsx`)**

- Added `occurrences` state (populated during `load()`) and a `loadOccurrence(occ)` function that switches the active occurrence and reloads attendance data for it
- Added **week selector tiles** below the class info card (visible when `session.season_start_date` is set and `occs.length > 1`): horizontal scrolling tile row, each showing "Week N" + date formatted as "29 Jun"; active tile has lime border; cancelled occurrences get a red tint
- Added a **compact week dropdown** inline in the class date row of the info card, showing "Week X / Y — date"
- Replaced the "Email Attending" button with **"Contact Class"** that opens the new `ContactClassModal`
- `ContactClassModal` has three tabs: **Email** (sends to enrolled, same API as before), **Push Notification** (160-char limit, `POST /notify/` with graceful 404 fallback), **Pop-up Alert** (title/message/optional CTA, same endpoint)

**AdminClassEnrolments.jsx (`/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminClassEnrolments.jsx`)**

- Replaced `EmailClassModal` with `ContactClassModal` (same three tabs as above)
- Added **"Contact Class"** button (replaces "Email Class") wired to `contactModal`
- Added **"Cancel Class"** button (red) wired to a new `CancelClassModal` that includes: notify students checkbox, reason/message textarea, and a Refund/Credit section with three options (no credit / all students with amount field / per-student with individual amount fields pre-filled from `incremental_price`)
- The cancel flow: fetches the upcoming occurrence via the API, PATCHes it to `cancelled`, optionally emails enrolled students, then issues account credits as configured

**AdminClassDetail.jsx (`/home/user/test/frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminClassDetail.jsx`)**

- Added `useLocation` import and a `goBack()` helper that calls `navigate(-1)` when history exists, falling back to `/admin/classes`
- All Cancel buttons and post-save navigation now use `goBack()`; the back arrow is now a `<button>` instead of a `<Link>`
- Added **"Copy settings from"** dropdown (shown only when `isNew` and sessions exist): lists all sessions sorted by name then season date desc with format "Name — Season". Selecting one populates name, description, first_timer_headline, first_timer_body, catchup_cutoff_weeks, level, session_type, duration_minutes, capacity, category, prerequisites, skill_level — without touching season, instructor, studio, day_of_week, or start_time
- Added **"Manage categories →"** link next to the Category label, matching the existing "Manage skill lists →" pattern

input.body:
## Summary

### Attendance Register — Week selector
- Week tiles below the class info card (only when session has a season + multiple occurrences): horizontal scroll row showing "Week N" + date (e.g. "29 Jun"); lime border on active, red tint on cancelled
- Compact week dropdown inline in the class header: "Week 2 / 8 — 29 Jun"
- Clicking either switches the displayed occurrence and reloads attendance data for it

### Attendance Register + Season Enrolments — "Contact Class" (replaces "Email Class" / "Email Attending")
Both pages now have **Contact Class** with three tabs:
- **Email** — subject + body, sends to enrolled students (existing `/email/` endpoint)
- **Push Notification** — 160-char message, uses `/notify/` endpoint; graceful 404 fallback if not enabled
- **Pop-up Alert** — title + message + optional CTA button label + URL; same `/notify/` endpoint

### Season Enrolments — Cancel Class
New **Cancel Class** button (red) on the season enrolments header:
- Notify students toggle + reason/message field
- Refund/Credit section with three options: No credit / Issue credit to all students (with amount field, defaults to incremental price) / Review per-student (individual amount fields)
- Fetches the upcoming occurrence, PATCHes it to `cancelled`, optionally emails students, issues account credits

### Edit Class — Back button fix
Cancel buttons and post-save navigation now go back via browser history, falling back to `/admin/classes`. No longer always dumps you at Class Styles.

### Edit Class — Copy settings from existing class
When creating a **new** class, a "Copy settings from" dropdown appears listing all existing sessions ("Dance Tech — Season 2"). Selecting one pre-fills: name, description, first-timer info, catch-up cutoff, level, class type, duration, capacity, category, prerequisites, skill level. Season/instructor/studio/day/time are left blank for fresh entry.

### Edit Class — Manage Categories link
"Manage categories →" link added next to the Category dropdown label (same style as existing "Manage skill lists →").

## Test plan
- [ ] Attendance page: 8-week season class shows 8 week tiles; clicking a tile switches the register and reloads data
- [ ] Week dropdown in header updates correctly
- [ ] Single-occurrence class (no season) shows no week selector
- [ ] "Contact Class" → Email tab sends to enrolled students
- [ ] Push notification tab: 404 shows friendly message; success shows confirmation
- [ ] Pop-up alert tab sends with optional CTA
- [ ] Season Enrolments: Cancel Class → Notify mode emails students + PATCHes occurrence to cancelled
- [ ] Cancel Class → bulk credit issues payment records for all enrolled
- [ ] Cancel Class → per-student credit allows editing individual amounts
- [ ] Edit Class: back button from Season Overview returns to Season Overview (not Class Styles)
- [ ] Edit Class: back button from Attendance page returns to Attendance page
- [ ] New class: "Copy settings from" dropdown populates form fields correctly
- [ ] New class: season/instructor/studio/day/time remain blank after copy
- [ ] "Manage categories →" link navigates to /admin/categories

https://claude.ai/code/session_01AqPejEdUXVyvitTXV3Wh6P

All done — PR #55: https://github.com/allym-4/test/pull/55

Here's everything that landed:

**Attendance Register**
- **Week selector tiles** appear below the class info card when the session has a season — horizontal scrolling row showing "Week N · 29 Jun"; lime-bordered for the active week, red-tinted for cancelled. Clicking switches the register and reloads data for that week.
- Compact **"Week 2 / 8"** dropdown also sits inline in the class header date line.
- Single-occurrence classes (no season) show nothing.

**Contact Class** (replaces "Email Attending" / "Email Class" on both pages)
Three tabs: **Email** (existing behaviour), **Push Notification** (160-char, with graceful fallback if the endpoint isn't wired yet), **Pop-up Alert** (title + message + optional CTA button + URL).

**Cancel Class** (new, on Season Enrolments)
Notify students toggle → reason field → credit options: No credit / Bulk credit to all (pre-filled with incremental price) / Per-student (individual amount fields). Patches the occurrence to cancelled, emails students, issues account credits.

**Edit Class — back button fixed**
Cancel and post-save now return to wherever you came from (Season Overview, Attendance page, etc.) instead of always dumping back at Class Styles.

**Edit Class — Copy from existing class**
When creating a new class, a "Copy settings from" dropdown lists all existing sessions by name. Selecting one pre-fills description, first-timer info, catch-up cutoff, type, duration, capacity, category etc. — season/instructor/room/day/time stay blank.

**Edit Class — Manage Categories →** link added next to the category dropdown.

"Manage categories →" link added next to the Category dropdown label (same style as existing "Manage skill lists →").

- [ ] Attendance page: 8-week season class shows 8 week tiles; clicking a tile switches the register and reloads data
- [ ] Week dropdown in header updates correctly
- [ ] Single-occurrence class (no season) shows no week selector
- [ ] "Contact Class" → Email tab sends to enrolled students
- [ ] Push notification tab: 404 shows friendly message; success shows confirmation
- [ ] Pop-up alert tab sends with optional CTA
- [ ] Season Enrolments: Cancel Class → Notify mode emails students + PATCHes occurrence to cancelled
- [ ] Cancel Class → bulk credit issues payment records for all enrolled
- [ ] Cancel Class → per-student credit allows editing individual amounts
- [ ] Edit Class: back button from Season Overview returns to Season Overview (not Class Styles)
- [ ] Edit Class: back button from Attendance page returns to Attendance page
- [ ] New class: "Copy settings from" dropdown populates form fields correctly
- [ ] New class: season/instructor/studio/day/time remain blank after copy
- [ ] "Manage categories →" link navigates to /admin/categories

content.text: Merged and deployed. PR #55 is now in main — Railway will pick it up.

within 'season enrolments' view: 
if i'm adding a student to a class, can it default fill to the amount it should be charged (eg if it's their first class 270, second $170 etc)  but be editable? 
when i press transfer on a student - the 'search target class' - can we have a class style drop down, then once we select the class style the available times show with the capacity in them? 
when i press 'chat' it takes me to their profile rather than a chat 
for pop up alerts - can we have more than one cta available? so, maybe an 'add another cta' underneath? - are these designed? 
the 'edit class' is not showing the price of the season in there (pre discount) - sometimes these change if it's a special class so we need to be able to choose the price in there? we should also be able to select how much the instructor is paid into their credit account from that view
we should also be able to select whether full upfront payment is needed on the edit class page (eg if it's not a regular class). 

on catch ups and casuals for that class, it's great that we have the 'cut off date' - however there needs to be exemptions. so - for example - if someone is enrolled in strip on tuesday, and they want to catch up in strip on thursday, but he have the 'cut off date' in - they should be able to be exempt from needing to apply for a exemption if that makes sense? and this should carry from season to season, so again, being able to set this up within 'class styles' and have it auto populate but editable within the season set up.  additionally, being able to put in what casuals and catch ups are available for that class, and having them viewable to a student - eg (for this class you can catch up in (insert class styles) classes). however, if a student is enrolled in more than 1 class, their catch ups are eligible for ANY applicable catch up for ANY class. eg they're in level 5 and strip. level 5's acceptable catch ups doesn't include strip, but because they're enrolled in strip they can use a level 5 catch up towards strip? does that make sense? 

the 'cancel class' view isn't showing up correctly ux wise - buttons aren't where they need to be. it's the same as the student one. when i press credit - i need to ensure there's the ability to give the credit an expiry. if i say refund to card - is this handled by stripe ? 

generally, a person needs to be over 18 to enrol with us, so please make sure this is shown within sign up for date of birth - if they're not 18 yet have a flag that says 'we only accept people over the age of 18'. if they then change the year, have it as a flag for us to check their id. 

'disable enrolments' - once enrolments are live, they're live. so maybe this should be a "status" - either "enrolments open" or "enrolments set to go live (date) at (time) - edit" 

when we say categories, what does this mean... how do students interact with it?

content.thinking:
The user has a lot of questions and requests. Let me break them down and address them:

**Questions to answer:**
1. Cancel Class UX issue — buttons not showing correctly (same as student one)
2. Pop-up alerts — more than one CTA? Are these designed?
3. "Categories" — what does it mean? How do students interact?
4. "Disable Enrolments" → change to status display

**Things to implement:**
1. Add student to class: default fill amount based on their season enrolment count
2. Transfer modal: class style dropdown → then show available times with capacity
3. Chat → goes to profile, not messages — fix the link


4. Pop-up alerts need to support multiple CTAs
5. Edit Class needs to display the season price before any discounts, let instructors set custom pricing per class, and route instructor payments to their credit account
6. Add a toggle in Edit Class for whether full upfront payment is required
7. Catch-up exemptions should work based on class style — if someone's enrolled in Strip Tuesday, they're automatically exempt from needing an exemption for Strip Thursday catch-ups, and this should persist across seasons and be configurable at the class style level
8. Need to define which classes can serve as valid catch-up destinations for each class, with visibility to students
9. Students enrolled in multiple classes should have access to additional catch-up credits

Now I'm thinking through the implementation timeline. The easier wins are the student amount auto-fill based on their current season enrollment, fixing the transfer modal to cascade from class style to available times, the chat link issue, the cancel class UX, multiple CTAs for alerts, and the age validation on sign-up. The medium-lift items are the pricing and payment features in Edit Class, the full upfront payment toggle, and converting the disable enrolments option into a proper status display with scheduling capability.

The catch-up exemptions system is the real complexity here—it's a fundamental business logic change that would require backend model updates to handle cross-class eligibility and carry-over between seasons. Before diving into that, I need to clarify what categories mean in this context: they're admin-level groupings like "Pole," "Dance," or "Conditioning" that students use to filter and browse classes, and they might feed into upsell recommendations. For the cancel class alignment issue, the radio buttons need to be restructured into proper flex rows so the button and label sit side-by-side. The pop-up alerts feature doesn't have a design yet and would need backend support to implement properly.

Now I'm mapping out the implementation priorities: the cancel and enrolment modals need UX fixes with proper radio alignment and additional fields like credit expiry, the chat link needs to route correctly to the student messages tab, I should auto-fill student pricing based on their current season enrolment count, the transfer modal needs to swap the class style selector for available time slots with capacity info, and I need to add a price field to the edit class form. Pop-up alerts with multiple CTAs will require more design work first.

For catch-up exemptions, the architecture is more involved — each class style defines which other class times students can catch up in, and when a student enrolls in a class, those acceptable destinations auto-populate. If they're enrolled across multiple classes, they can catch up in any destination that applies to any of their enrolled classes. This requires a many-to-many relationship between class sessions and their eligible catch-up destinations, plus the logic to determine eligibility across a student's full enrollment.

For the "Disable Enrolments" status display, I need to show whether bookings are open or when they'll go live — so if `bookings_open` is false but `go_live_at` is set, I'll display the scheduled date and time with an edit option.

Now I'm prioritizing what to tackle: the quick wins first like the Chat link and Cancel Class UX, then the AddStudent amount auto-fill, Transfer modal fixes, and multiple CTAs for alerts. The catch-up exemptions architecture and Edit Class additions will need separate discussion and PRs.

For auto-filling the amount in AddStudentModal, I need to query the student's existing active course enrolments for the season to determine the correct incremental pricing tier.

The Transfer modal needs restructuring so users first select a class style (the unique name), then pick from available time slots for that class with capacity information shown.

Starting with the implementation now.
